lenses.guidance: vv 23-29 mark the structural pivot of the book: Moses begs to cross over, is denied, and shows Joshua the land from Pisgah. The narrative turn — leader stays, people enter — frames everything that follows.

v 26's 'the LORD was angry with me on your account' picks up the Num 20:12 disqualification and threads it through Ps 106:32 — the canonical lament for Moses's barred entry. The mediator falls short of the rest he proclaims.

vv 27-28's Pisgah-view command — 'look... but you will not cross' — is the redemption arc's bittersweet hinge. The promise is seen but not entered by Moses; Joshua's leading anticipates a greater rest still to come.
